name: How Do I Prepare My House for a New Flooring Installation?

step.name: Step 1
step.text: You've picked out the perfect new floors—congratulations! The next step is getting your home ready for installation day. A little preparation goes a long way toward ensuring a smooth, efficient process. First, you'll need to completely clear the rooms. This includes all furniture, rugs, plants, and any other items on the floor. It's also a good idea to remove valuables and fragile decor from the walls. Talk to your contractor beforehand to clarify whether they include furniture moving in their quote.

step.name: Step 2
step.text: Next, plan for the delivery of your flooring materials. Most wood and laminate products need to acclimate to your home's environment for 48 to 72 hours before they can be installed. This means the boxes will need to be stored in the actual installation room with your heat or AC running as usual. Don't leave them in the garage! Finally, discuss the layout with your installers before they begin. Confirm the direction the planks will run and how transitions between rooms will be handled. Taking these simple steps helps prevent surprises and ensures your project starts off on the right foot.

step.name: Step 3
step.text: Getting a home ready for a flooring project can be a lot to manage. If you'd rather leave it all to an expert, City Dweller, LLC offers end-to-end services to handle every detail for you. Call us for a free quote!

description: How Do I Prepare My House for a New Flooring Installation?
